summary: base58-utils ships an encrypted dropper that decodes and executes a hidden second stage on require, with crontab persistence
details: The package advertises Base58 encoding utilities but its index.js contains an on-load routine that, 42 seconds after require(), AES-256-CBC decrypts a 53KB base64 blob disguised as test/fixtures/keypairs.dat using a hardcoded key and IV, writes the resulting JavaScript to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0o700, and launches it via child_process.spawn('node', [syncd]) detached and unref'd. The same routine installs a user crontab entry that re-executes ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js every 12 hours via /usr/bin/node, providing persistence independent of the calling program. Identifiers are obfuscated (_K, _I, _s, _fs, _cp), the payload is stored under a test-fixture path, and execution is delayed to evade immediate observation. The decrypted content is opaque attacker-controlled JavaScript unrelated to Base58 encoding.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
